% --- T1_3_temporal_split_statistics.tex ---
\begin{table}
\caption{Temporal Split Strategy: Date Ranges, Record Counts, and Purpose}
\label{tab:temporal_splits}
\begin{tabular}{lllrlll}
\toprule
Split & Weeks & Date Range & Days & Segment Records & Trip Records & Purpose \\
\midrule
Train & W1, W2, W3 & Jul 29 - Aug 18 & 21 & 289,331 & 7,598 & XGBoost model training \\
Calibration & W4 & Aug 19 - Aug 25 & 7 & 104,655 & 2,740 & Conformal calibration (nonconformity scores) \\
Test Near & W5 & Aug 26 - Sep 01 & 7 & 103,700 & 2,707 & Test: minimal temporal drift \\
Test Mid & W6 & Sep 02 - Sep 08 & 5 & 69,537 & 1,833 & Test: moderate temporal drift \\
Test Far & W7, W8 & Sep 09 - Sep 21 & 13 & 180,575 & 4,736 & Test: maximum temporal drift \\
\bottomrule
\end{tabular}
\end{table}



% --- T2_1_feature_summary.tex ---
\begin{table}
\caption{Feature set summary for route-level and segment-level models. Historical features use strict 7-day past-only lookback to prevent data leakage.}
\label{tab:feature_summary}
\begin{tabular}{llllp{5cm}l}
\toprule
Feature & Type & Level & Description & Source \\
\midrule
hour_of_day & Temporal & Both & Hour of day (0-23) & start_time \\
minute_of_day & Temporal & Both & Minute of day (0-1439) & start_time \\
day_of_week & Temporal & Both & Day of week (0=Mon, 6=Sun) & date \\
is_weekend & Temporal & Both & Weekend indicator (0/1) & day_of_week \\
hour_sin & Temporal & Both & Cyclical sin encoding of hour (period=24) & hour_of_day \\
hour_cos & Temporal & Both & Cyclical cos encoding of hour (period=24) & hour_of_day \\
dow_sin & Temporal & Both & Cyclical sin encoding of day (period=7) & day_of_week \\
dow_cos & Temporal & Both & Cyclical cos encoding of day (period=7) & day_of_week \\
route_id_encoded & Spatial & Both & Label-encoded route identifier & route_short_name \\
direction_encoded & Spatial & Both & Binary direction indicator (0/1) & direction \\
hist_route_mean & Historical & Route & Mean travel time from past 7 days & 7d lookback \\
hist_route_std & Historical & Route & Std dev of travel time from past 7 days & 7d lookback \\
hist_route_median & Historical & Route & Median travel time from past 7 days & 7d lookback \\
hist_route_q25 & Historical & Route & 25th percentile from past 7 days & 7d lookback \\
hist_route_q75 & Historical & Route & 75th percentile from past 7 days & 7d lookback \\
hist_route_count & Historical & Route & Number of observations in past 7 days & 7d lookback \\
hist_seg_mean & Historical & Segment & Mean run time from past 7 days for segment & 7d lookback \\
hist_seg_std & Historical & Segment & Std dev of run time from past 7 days & 7d lookback \\
hist_seg_median & Historical & Segment & Median run time from past 7 days & 7d lookback \\
hist_seg_q25 & Historical & Segment & 25th percentile from past 7 days & 7d lookback \\
hist_seg_q75 & Historical & Segment & 75th percentile from past 7 days & 7d lookback \\
hist_seg_count & Historical & Segment & Number of observations in past 7 days & 7d lookback \\
segment & Spatial & Segment & Segment number along route & raw data \\
segment_number_normalized & Spatial & Segment & Segment position normalized to [0, 1] & segment / max_segment \\
total_route_segments & Spatial & Segment & Total segments in this trip route & trip_id groupby \\
cumulative_time_so_far & Trip Progress & Segment & Cumulative time of preceding segments (s) & running sum \\
segments_completed & Trip Progress & Segment & Number of segments already traversed & cumcount \\
fraction_route_completed & Trip Progress & Segment & Fraction of route completed (0-1) & segments_completed / total \\
prev_seg_run_time & Lag & Segment & Run time of segment (n-1) & shift(1) \\
prev_seg_dwell_time & Lag & Segment & Dwell time of segment (n-1) & shift(1) \\
prev_2_seg_avg_run_time & Lag & Segment & Avg run time of segments (n-1), (n-2) & shift(1,2) \\
prev_3_seg_avg_run_time & Lag & Segment & Avg run time of segments (n-1) to (n-3) & shift(1,2,3) \\
\bottomrule
\end{tabular}
\end{table}

% --- T4_4_significance.tex ---
\begin{table}
\caption{Statistical Significance Tests for Coverage Degradation (RQ1)}
\label{tab:t4_4_significance}
\begin{tabular}{lllll}
\toprule
Comparison & Test & Statistic & p-value & Conclusion \\
\midrule
Calibration vs. 0.90 & Binomial (two-sided) & 2468/2740 & 9.2391e-01 & Not significant \\
Test Near vs. 0.90 & Binomial (two-sided) & 1670/2707 & 0.0000e+00 & Significant (p<0.05) \\
Test Mid vs. 0.90 & Binomial (two-sided) & 1105/1833 & 4.2418e-246 & Significant (p<0.05) \\
Test Far vs. 0.90 & Binomial (two-sided) & 2875/4736 & 0.0000e+00 & Significant (p<0.05) \\
Test Near vs. Test Mid & Chi-squared (2x2) & chi2=0.85 & 3.5548e-01 & Not significant \\
Test Near vs. Test Far & Chi-squared (2x2) & chi2=0.66 & 4.1501e-01 & Not significant \\
Test Mid vs. Test Far & Chi-squared (2x2) & chi2=0.08 & 7.7530e-01 & Not significant \\
Calibration vs. Test Far & Chi-squared (2x2) & chi2=732.73 & 2.2820e-161 & Significant (p<0.05) \\
\bottomrule
\end{tabular}
\end{table}

% --- T7_1_master_results.tex ---
\begin{table}
\caption{Master Results Table: All Methods Across All Experiments}
\label{tab:master_results}
\begin{tabular}{llccccl}
\toprule
Experiment & Method & Overall PICP & Overall MPIW & Cal. Error & Winkler & Key Finding \\
\midrule
Exp1 & Static CP & 0.6091 & 1528.3115 & 0.2909 & 9544.5869 & Coverage degrades with temporal distance from calibration (RQ1) \\
Exp2 & Online Expanding & 0.7462 & 2163.8015 & 0.1538 & 7464.9670 & Growing calibration set stabilizes coverage under drift (RQ2) \\
Exp2 & Online Sliding-7d & 0.6547 & 1626.3179 & 0.2453 & 8728.9015 & Sliding window adapts fastest to distribution shifts (RQ2) \\
Exp2 & Online Sliding-14d & 0.7207 & 2018.0930 & 0.1793 & 7843.1981 & NaN \\
Exp3 & Segment Aggregated & 0.9828 & 4132.0849 & 0.0828 & 4254.4537 & Aggregated segment intervals preserve route-level coverage (RQ3) \\
Exp3 & Segment Bonferroni & 1.0000 & 18122.2949 & 0.1000 & 18122.2949 & Bonferroni correction ensures conservative coverage at route level (RQ3) \\
\bottomrule
\end{tabular}
\end{table}



% --- T7_2_contributions.tex ---
\begin{table}
\caption{Thesis Contributions Summary}
\label{tab:contributions}
\begin{tabular}{p{4.5cm}p{5.5cm}p{5.5cm}}
\toprule
Contribution & Evidence & Significance \\
\midrule
C1: Empirical characterization of conformal prediction under temporal distribution shift in bus transit & Exp1: Static CP coverage degrades from Near to Far test periods; KS tests confirm non-stationarity across weeks & First systematic study of conformal prediction calibration decay in bus transit domain; quantifies the temporal validity horizon \\
C2: Comparative evaluation of online adaptive conformal methods for transit ETA & Exp2: Online Sliding-7d achieves most stable coverage across all periods; Online Expanding balances adaptivity with sample size & Demonstrates that simple online updates restore coverage guarantees without model retraining; practical for real-time deployment \\
C3: Segment-level uncertainty decomposition with conformal guarantees & Exp3: Segment Aggregated method enables spatial uncertainty attribution while Bonferroni correction ensures conservative route-level coverage & Novel application of segment-level conformal prediction to transit; enables actionable interpretability for transit operators \\
C4: Practical framework for uncertainty-aware bus ETA prediction & All experiments: Complete pipeline from data cleaning through conformal prediction with XGBoost, validated on 55 days of real-world Astana bus data & Reproducible methodology applicable to other cities and transit modes; bridges gap between ML uncertainty quantification and transit operations \\
\bottomrule
\end{tabular}
\end{table}
